review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: Working as Software Engineer in LTIMindtree

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: There are hardly any pros with this company. Before the merger, "Mindtree" was a great place for freshers. I originally joined as a Mindtree employee.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: There are many downsides, including issues with training, projects, and salary hikes. Before joining, I had heard that Mindtree offered excellent training for freshers, but in reality, it’s the opposite. The training materials are outdated, and there are no good mentors during internships. In our experience, they conducted a foundation exam, but it held no real value as we were randomly assigned to MERN, MEAN, Java, or .NET tracks. During the internship, they provided outdated pre-recorded training materials, mostly from YouTube and another platform related to our assigned domains. Afterward, they conducted a demo project review as the final step of the internship. A batch of qualified candidates was onboarded, but many were left stranded. A few months later, another batch of 500 people was onboarded, while the remaining batch was never onboarded. Those who did get onboarded went through basic foundation training in Java and DBMS, and were again asked to create a demo project for review, which was delayed by several months. After that, very few people were assigned to projects, which were mostly support roles; development projects were rare. I was personally on the bench for a year. Because of the long bench period and lack of projects, many of my colleagues who joined with me were asked to resign. Therefore, it is advisable not to join as a fresher. Work life balance will depend on the project and pay hike is very low here.

review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: I'll never suggest to join LTIMindtree
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: There isn't anything in particular to like.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
The work culture in the Microsoft CSS project at LTIMindtree is very poor. There is excessive micromanagement, and work-life balance is non-existent. The benefits are lacking, and when it comes to appraisals, the average increase is just 0-2 percent, so there's little room for growth. Promotions are handled internally, without any corresponding salary hike or change in salary grade or designation. Instead, employees receive a simple email from management stating that they will be taking on a new role, such as a Technical Advisor.

If you are a fresher or a developer and receive an offer from LTIMindtree for the Microsoft CSS Technical Support project, I would strongly advise against joining this organization.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: The company is an established MNC, which one would expect to be reliable.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: It's been a concerning nine months since I last received any updates. Queries submitted to their helpdesk have gone unanswered, and attempts to reach HR by phone have been unsuccessful. This has been an incredibly frustrating experience. On the positive side, the company is an established MNC, which one would expect to be reliable. However, when it comes to the negatives, it's hard to know where to begin. Initially, I was hired for a Level 2 position with a promised salary of 6.5 LPA. I received the offer letter, but since then, there have been no further updates. I'm simply looking for information regarding my joining date. If they've decided to rescind the offer, I'd appreciate knowing that as well. Another candidate and I, both selected, have been left in the dark. This turn of events is surprising, especially considering that LTI is a well-known MNC and has consistently recruited from our college in previous years. While I can't advise against applying to this company, I do hope you'll consider other options. Perhaps focus on product-based companies or those offering in-office internships, if possible.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Call centre kind of a job. You will work for Microsoft as a support engineer. But this job role will involve everything that a call centre worker does.
No training is being provided since 2 years. New joiners are struggling very badly because of this.
90% of the project involves night shift.
Target based job and the targets are extremely unrealistic. 
CSAT/5 star reviews from customers will decide how much time you can work here. These reviews rely on hard work as well as you luck. Overall services of Microsoft are deteriorating day by day which is why customers have stopped giving 5 stars for the tickets. Thus, luck factor comes into play.
If you are not very lucky, and end up getting few 4 stars out of 5, in that case you may get fired. Managers will harass you every day for getting a 4 star. If you get a 1 star, manager will abuse you on call(using Maa behen slangs).
Managers/Project managers belong to BPO industry which creates an understanding gap between you and them. They lack competency to understand the technicalities involve in dealing with technical tickets. 
Higher management is even more Pathetic. They just care about revenue and profit.
Leadership team is just for name sake.
HRs are very very rude and inconsiderate. They will reply to your concerns after 7-10 days. Nobody cares if you are suffering from any medical illness.
You will not have any respect in the company.
Used to have regular issues with salary after the merger of LTI and Mindtree.
The young peers are graduates of engineering colleges. However, experienced ones are 12th pass, non technical graduates. Most of them have worked in call centres. This job is not meant for the engineers at all. It is very shameful and disrespectful to your career if you opt this job. I would request the young people/freshers to stay away from this job and look for a software engineering/testing/development job which reward in the later phase of your career.

review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: Exemplary Project Management Excellence: A Review of LTImindtree
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
As a project manager, I have had the privilege of collaborating with LTImindtree on a recent project, and I am delighted to share my experience. From the outset, LTImindtree demonstrated a remarkable commitment to excellence, professionalism, and client satisfaction.

First and foremost, I was impressed by the proactive approach taken by the LTImindtree team. Their project management strategies were meticulously planned and flawlessly executed, ensuring that every aspect of the project remained on track and within budget. Communication was seamless, with regular updates provided at each stage of the project lifecycle.

Furthermore, LTImindtree exhibited unparalleled expertise in their field. Their team members were not only highly skilled but also possessed a deep understanding of the latest industry trends and best practices. This expertise translated into innovative solutions that exceeded our expectations and added significant value to the project.

One of the standout qualities of LTImindtree was their unwavering commitment to client satisfaction. Throughout the project, they consistently went above and beyond to address our needs and concerns promptly. Their dedication to delivering results of the highest quality was evident in every interaction, making them a trusted partner we could rely on.

In summary, working with LTImindtree as a project manager has been an exceptional experience. Their exemplary project management practices, combined with their expertise and dedication to client satisfaction, make them a standout choice for any project. I wholeheartedly recommend LTImindtree to anyone seeking a reliable and proficient partner for their business endeavors.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:

Communication Clarity: While communication with LTImindtree was generally effective, there were occasional lapses in clarity, requiring clarification and reiteration to ensure all stakeholders were on the same page.

Proactive Feedback Implementation: While LTImindtree's dedication to client satisfaction is commendable, there were moments when feedback implementation could have been more proactive, enhancing their already impressive customer service.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: There are several potential pros for working in an organization. Here are some common advantages: Professional Development: Organizations often provide opportunities for professional growth and skill development. They may offer training programs, mentorship opportunities, and access to resources that can help you enhance your knowledge and expertise. Stability and Job Security: Established organizations generally offer more stability and job security compared to start-ups or freelance work. They often have established client bases, financial stability, and long-term plans, which can provide a sense of security for employees. Benefits and Compensation: Many organizations provide competitive salaries, benefits packages, and additional perks. These benefits may include health insurance, retirement plans, paid time off, flexible working hours, and various employee discounts. Collaboration and Teamwork: Working in an organization allows you to collaborate with colleagues from diverse backgrounds and areas of expertise. This collaboration fosters teamwork, knowledge sharing, and the opportunity to learn from others, which can lead to personal and professional growth. Networking Opportunities: Organizations provide an environment for building professional networks. By working with colleagues, clients, and stakeholders, you can expand your network and create connections that may be valuable for future career opportunities.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: Bureaucracy and Hierarchy: Large organizations often have complex bureaucratic structures and hierarchies that can slow down decision-making processes. This can result in increased levels of red tape and limited autonomy in decision-making for employees. Limited Flexibility: Organizational settings may have more rigid work schedules and limited flexibility compared to other work arrangements. This can restrict your ability to have a flexible work-life balance or accommodate personal commitments.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Depending upon your project, you can learn a lot.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
- Post merging with LTI, the company has been on a downward spiral. All policies have been revised to match those of LTI. 
- Compensation cycle has become a joke. It disincentivizes hardworking employees. This year's cycle was delayed by 4 months without any clarity being provided as to when it will happen. When it did happen, most employees received a 0% increment. Hard working employees who received stellar ratings from both their managers and their customers received single digit increments. All this while, employees were bombarded with mails detailing how well the company was doing, how many awards we were getting, and how great the increase in YoY revenue was. 
- Communication from upper management is terrible. The ambiguity leaves most employees frustrated. Resolution of any request takes an inordinate amount of time.
- In case an employee resigns, the management is unnecessarily slow in informing the customer about it. When one of my team members resigned, the customer was informed about it 3 days before he left. None of us were allowed to tell the customer about it, to ensure that we didn't cause a "panic".
- Employee benefits have been stripped to the bone with the merger. Leaves have been reduced from 20 to 15 per year, and instead of being credited at the start of the year, they are credited on a monthly basis. To make matters worse, any leave greater than 2 days must be approved 2 months in advance, which is not always feasible.
- It is now mandatory to come to office two days a week. Also, as per LTI policy, leaves shall be credited only if this weekly quota is met. However, management claims that the number of employees is greater than the existing seating capacity, so we need to book our seats. The seat booking application is terrible and shows that there are no more seats available even though more than half the office is empty on any given day. So, if you forget to book your seats well in advance, your leaves for the entire month may be revoked.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Recruiter & HR only try to lure you with the name of the big brand
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
I wish to share an incident that occurred during my recruitment process for the position of Principal Architect. The process commenced on July 19, 2023, with the clear understanding that my current CTC is XX lakhs, and my expectation would be YY+ lakhs. However, the recruiter informed me that there is a tight budget of only XX+2 lakhs per annum, to which I agreed. 

Subsequently, I successfully completed all technical interviews and provided verifications for all ITRs, PF, educational documents, Experience Letters, and salary slips from my previous 16 years of employment.

Finally, on August 14, 2023, I participated in the HR interview. During this interaction, I informed HR that I am located in my home town and would require an adjustment in my expectation to YY+ lakhs, considering relocation and other expenses. She requested details of my compensation package for the past 2 years and assured me that she would verify this with the business before scheduling another call. 

On August 16, 2023, I was taken aback by the package offered to me, which was XX-5 lakhs. I find it perplexing how this could be the case, as my current salary is XX lakhs, and I had communicated an expectation of YY lakhs. Recruiter provided the following explanation: "Over the past 2.3 years, you have changed companies three times, resulting in a substantial increase in your salary. We cannot exceed the offer we have already shared."

 In response, I replied to her email as follows: "I regret to express that I consider this to be unprofessional conduct. This is the first instance of encountering such behavior in my 16 years of experience. This should have been addressed as the initial step before commencing the interview process, ensuring clarity from the outset. We initiated this process with an agreement at XX+2 lakhs per annum, and subsequent to my assessment of relocation and other expenses, I believe the appropriate figure should be YY lakhs. Consequently, your offer should be a minimum of XX+2 lakhs per annum, as previously agreed." 

And I have written communication that we agreed on.

Innovative Projects: LTIMindtree often works on cutting-edge technology projects, offering employees a chance to work with the latest technologies and innovative solutions.
Learning and Development: The company has a strong focus on learning and development, providing various resources and programs for employees to upskill and advance their careers.
Diverse Work Environment: With a global presence, LTIMindtree offers exposure to diverse cultures and global working environments, fostering a multicultural and inclusive workplace.
Career Opportunities: Being a large organization, it provides a wide range of career paths and opportunities for vertical and lateral movement within the company.
Employee Benefits: Competitive salary packages, health benefits, and work-life balance initiatives (depending on the region and role) can be attractive aspects of working at LTIMindtree.

Resource Management: There may be instances of uneven resource distribution, where some teams are stretched thin while others might not be fully utilized.
Communication Gaps: Given its size and global presence, there can be communication gaps between different levels of management and teams, affecting project coordination and employee engagement.
Integration Challenges: Following the merger that created LTIMindtree, there may be ongoing integration challenges as the organization continues to merge processes, cultures, and technologies from L&T Infotech and Mindtree.

You will only book 40 hours/week, regardless if you worked more, significantly more. You will not receive comp time. You will have to frequently clear your browser to navigate LTIM systems. You will not be able to copy/paste/download throughout most of LTIM systems. You are working for a third world company, you are a third world employee, US labor laws are stretched. You will have to complete annual trainings from both the client and LTIM on Cyber Security/Harassment/Privacy, etc. How many times do you have to be told not to grab someone's butt, to not respond to spam, to not trade client stocks? Financial trainings will be in rupees. If on bench you might as well be in an isolation tank, communication is horrible, rude, and overly demanding. You may or may not receive bonuses, certainly not on a regular basis, so don't count on it. Insurance/benefits are so-so. You will be spammed with irrelevant emails, dozens daily. You will become unbelievably frustrated. You will not have work/life balance, you won't even have work balance. RUN!!!
